meta_title: Roofing in Lauderdale Manors, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated roofing in Lauderdale Manors, FL. CertainTeed & West Lake systems. HVHZ-compliant, licensed & insured. Free estimates!
h1: Trusted Roofing Replacements & Repairs in Lauderdale Manors, FL
intro:
Lauderdale Manors is a tight-knit Broward County community where pride of ownership runs deep — and where the roof over your head is one of the most critical investments you can make. Nestled just four miles from the Atlantic coast, this 33311 zip code neighborhood sits squarely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), meaning every roofing system installed here must meet the most rigorous building standards in the entire state. With an average home value of around $260,000, protecting your property with a properly rated, professionally installed roof isn't just smart — it's essential for preserving what you've worked hard to own.

About 65% of the homes in Lauderdale Manors were built before 1994, placing them in a particularly vulnerable category when it comes to roofing performance. Pre-1994 construction largely predates the sweeping reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew, meaning tens of thousands of square feet of roofing in this neighborhood were never engineered to withstand today's 170 mph wind zone requirements. Whether your home sits near Lauderdale Manors Park, along the residential streets bordering Fort Lauderdale and Oakland Park, or deeper into the Lauderhill corridor, the reality is the same: aging roofing materials, outdated installation methods, and decades of South Florida sun and storm exposure add up to serious risk.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand Lauderdale Manors because we've worked in this community and neighborhoods just like it throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We bring 60+ years of combined installation experience to every project, and every roof we install is completed entirely by our in-house team — no subcontractors, no shortcuts. Whether you're dealing with wind damage from a recent storm, persistent leaks that just won't quit, or a roof that's simply reached the end of its service life, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is the partner Lauderdale Manors homeowners trust to get it done right, the first time.

section_why.h2: Why Lauderdale Manors Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Roof
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in a community like Lauderdale Manors means choosing someone who truly understands the local environment, the building code landscape, and what it takes to keep a South Florida home protected for decades to come. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has earned an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and has completed roofing projects on more than 500 homes across Broward County and surrounding areas — not by cutting corners, but by delivering consistent, high-quality workmanship backed by real accountability.

One of our most important differentiators is our 100% in-house installation model. Many contractors in the area rely heavily on subcontracted labor, which introduces variability in quality and makes it harder to ensure proper HVHZ compliance on every single detail of a roof installation. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, every crew member who steps onto your Lauderdale Manors roof is a trained, vetted, direct employee of our company. That means consistent quality control from the first tear-off nail to the final ridge cap — every single time.

With 60+ years of combined experience among our installation teams, we've seen everything South Florida roofing can throw at a contractor — from post-hurricane emergency replacements to complex tile re-roofs on older Broward County homes. We know how to properly handle permit submissions through the Broward County Building Division, how to conduct thorough deck inspections on aging pre-1994 homes, and how to select the right roofing system for each home's specific exposure and budget. We also work with homeowners who are navigating insurance claims, helping document damage and ensure your new roof qualifies for the wind mitigation credits you deserve.

Financing is another area where Premier Impact Windows & Roofing stands apart. We know that a full roof replacement can feel financially daunting, especially for working families in Lauderdale Manors. That's why we offer flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available — so protecting your home doesn't have to mean emptying your savings. Our team will walk you through every option clearly, with no pressure and no hidden fees.

section_history.h2: Lauderdale Manors Has Weathered the Storms — Your Roof Should Too
section_history.body:
Broward County has absorbed some of Florida's most damaging hurricanes over the past three decades, and Lauderdale Manors has felt that impact firsthand. Since 1990, this area has been touched by at least 10 named storms of varying intensity. The most notorious — Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — reshaped the entire landscape of Florida roofing standards. Andrew's catastrophic destruction across South Florida exposed just how inadequate pre-existing building codes were, leading to the comprehensive overhaul that established the modern Florida Building Code and HVHZ requirements. But homes built before those reforms, which represent the majority of the Lauderdale Manors housing stock, were never retroactively upgraded.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought another round of widespread roof damage to Broward County. As one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes on record, Wilma generated sustained winds and pressure drops that peeled back roofing systems across the region, revealing how many older homes still had vulnerable roof-to-wall connections and undersized fastener patterns. Then in 2017, Hurricane Irma reinforced the lesson once again — sweeping through South Florida with enough sustained force to expose weak points in roofing systems of all ages. Homes that had been patched and repaired after prior storms often fared worse than those with fully replaced, code-compliant roofs.

The pattern is clear: Lauderdale Manors homes face real, recurring storm risk, and older roofing systems simply cannot be expected to perform reliably through another major hurricane season. A properly installed, HVHZ-rated roof isn't a luxury upgrade — it's the single most important structural defense your home has against the storms that are certain to return to Broward County in the years ahead.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & HVHZ Roofing Requirements in Lauderdale Manors
section_code.body:
Lauderdale Manors falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, a designation that applies to Miami-Dade and Broward counties and carries the most stringent roofing installation and product approval requirements in the country. Under the Florida Building Code, every roofing product installed in an HVHZ jurisdiction must carry a Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) — a rigorous product-level certification that goes well beyond the standard approvals required in other parts of Florida or the nation. This means that not just any shingle or tile product can legally be installed on a Lauderdale Manors home, and not just any contractor is equipped to meet these standards.

The 170 mph wind zone designation for the 33311 area further dictates how roofing systems must be fastened, sealed, and connected to the underlying structure. This includes specific requirements for nail patterns, underlayment systems, hip and ridge applications, and flashing details around penetrations. For tile roofs, HVHZ code mandates specific mortar or adhesive application methods and uplift resistance testing. These aren't optional enhancements — they are legal minimums that every permitted roofing job in Lauderdale Manors must satisfy.

All roofing permits in Lauderdale Manors are processed through the Broward County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application submission to final inspection sign-off. We maintain up-to-date licensing and insurance as required by Broward County, and our installations are designed to pass inspection the first time. We never ask homeowners to navigate the permit system alone or, worse, suggest skipping the permit process — because an unpermitted roof in an HVHZ jurisdiction can create serious problems when it comes time to sell your home or file an insurance claim.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed & West Lake Roofing Systems Built for Lauderdale Manors Conditions
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two premium roofing product lines that are fully rated for HVHZ use and ideally suited to the climate and aesthetic character of Lauderdale Manors homes. For homeowners seeking an architectural shingle solution, we install CertainTeed's Landmark Series — one of the most respected names in residential roofing. The Landmark Series features a dual-layered, fiberglass-reinforced construction that delivers exceptional wind resistance, impact protection, and long-term dimensional stability. CertainTeed Landmark shingles are available in a wide range of colors and profiles to complement everything from classic Broward County ranch homes to more contemporary builds, and they carry the ASTM D3161 Class F wind resistance rating alongside Miami-Dade approval for use in HVHZ applications.

For homeowners who prefer the timeless elegance and exceptional longevity of tile roofing, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. West Lake tiles are engineered specifically with Florida's demanding climate in mind, offering superior resistance to wind-driven rain, UV degradation, and the constant thermal cycling that accelerates wear in South Florida. Both the concrete and clay tile options from West Lake carry full Miami-Dade NOA certification, making them fully compliant for installation in Lauderdale Manors and throughout Broward County. Clay tile in particular offers outstanding longevity — often 40 to 50 years or more — making it one of the most cost-effective long-term roofing investments available.

Whether you choose CertainTeed architectural shingles or a West Lake tile system, every Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof installation includes HVHZ-compliant underlayment, proper flashing, and fastening systems that meet or exceed Florida Building Code requirements. We help you choose the right product for your home's structure, your neighborhood aesthetic, and your long-term goals — so your new Lauderdale Manors roof performs beautifully for decades.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Lauderdale Manors — and How Do You Pay for It?
section_cost.body:
For homes in Lauderdale Manors, where the average property value sits around $260,000, a full roof replacement is one of the highest-return investments a homeowner can make. The cost of a new roof in this area varies based on home size, roof pitch, material selection, and the condition of the existing deck — but most full replacements using CertainTeed Landmark shingles or West Lake tile systems fall within a range that is well within the scope of financing. Given the insurance savings, storm protection value, and potential impact on home resale, the investment typically pays for itself faster than most homeowners expect.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options specifically designed for working-class and middle-income homeowners. Plans start as low as $87 per month, and we offer $0 down options for qualified homeowners so you don't have to delay critical roof work while saving up a large lump sum. A roof that's failing or storm-damaged only gets more expensive with time — water intrusion can compromise roof decking, attic insulation, drywall, and even structural framing if left unaddressed. Getting the roof replaced now, on a manageable monthly payment, is almost always the more financially sound decision.

Beyond the direct protection value, a new HVHZ-compliant roof can meaningfully increase your home's market value and appeal to buyers in the Lauderdale Manors area. Real estate professionals throughout Broward County consistently note that roof age and condition are among the top factors buyers scrutinize — and a newly replaced, permitted, code-compliant roof can make a significant difference at closing time. Combined with the insurance premium savings detailed in the next section, the total financial case for a new roof in Lauderdale Manors is compelling.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Lauderdale Manors Can Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance
section_insurance.body:
Homeowner's insurance in Broward County is among the most expensive in the nation, and for good reason — the storm exposure is real and the claim history is substantial. But what many Lauderdale Manors homeowners don't realize is that a new, properly installed, HVHZ-rated roof can significantly reduce their annual insurance premiums through wind mitigation credits. Florida law requires insurers to offer discounts when a home demonstrates measurable wind resistance improvements, and a new roof with compliant fastening, sealed underlayment, and rated materials is one of the most powerful wind mitigation improvements you can make.

After Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs your new roof, you'll be eligible to have a licensed wind mitigation inspector assess your home and complete an OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Report — the official form used by Florida insurers to calculate your discount. Homes with HVHZ-rated roofs that meet all current Florida Building Code requirements for roof covering, deck attachment, and roof-to-wall connection can see annual insurance savings ranging from hundreds to over a thousand dollars per year, depending on your carrier and current policy structure. Common carriers operating in Broward County all recognize these credits as required by the state.

For older Lauderdale Manors homes — particularly those built before 1994 — the transition from a pre-code roof to a fully rated, HVHZ-compliant system can be especially dramatic in terms of insurance impact. Some insurers have even begun non-renewing or excluding wind coverage on homes with aging roofs past a certain threshold. Replacing your roof proactively not only keeps your coverage intact but positions you to negotiate better rates and terms. Our team is experienced in working alongside insurance adjusters and wind mitigation inspectors to ensure your new Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof delivers every dollar of credit you're entitled to.

faqs.q: Do most homes in Lauderdale Manors need a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: Given that approximately 65% of homes in Lauderdale Manors were built before 1994, many properties are working with roofing systems that are well past their expected service life. While not every situation requires a full replacement, homes with multiple layers of shingles, significant granule loss, recurring leaks, or compromised roof decking are typically better served by a full replacement. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides honest, no-pressure assessments to help you understand exactly what your roof needs.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Lauderdale Manors, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Roof replacement costs in Lauderdale Manors vary based on roof size, pitch, and materials chosen — CertainTeed Landmark shingles and West Lake tile systems are both competitively priced for the quality they deliver. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified homeowners. We provide transparent, itemized estimates so you know exactly what you're paying for before any work begins.

faqs.q: Does my new roof in Lauderdale Manors need to be permitted through Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes — all roofing replacements in Lauderdale Manors require a permit through the Broward County Building Division, and the installation must comply with HVHZ standards under the Florida Building Code. Skipping permits is never a legal or smart option; unpermitted roofs can invalidate insurance coverage and create serious complications during home sales. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permitting on your behalf from start to final inspection sign-off.

faqs.q: How long does a full roof replacement take in Lauderdale Manors?
faqs.a: Most standard residential roof replacements in Lauderdale Manors can be completed within one to three days once materials are staged and permits are approved. The overall timeline from contract signing to completed installation typically ranges from one to three weeks, depending on permit processing times through the Broward County Building Division and material availability. We coordinate the schedule to minimize disruption to your household and keep you informed at every stage.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida law requires insurers to provide wind mitigation discounts for homes that meet current HVHZ roofing standards. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing roof is installed, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can complete the required OIR-B1-1802 report documenting your roof's upgraded ratings. Depending on your carrier and prior roof condition, annual savings can range from several hundred to over a thousand dollars, often making a major dent in your monthly financing payment.
